conjecture_title: Size of symmetric intersecting families in $[k]^n$
paper_title: On symmetric intersecting families of vectors
summary: Eberhard, Kahn, Narayanan, and Spirkl (arXiv:1909.11578) proved that any symmetric intersecting $A \subset [k]^n$ has size $o(k^n)$ for fixed $k \geq 3$, but the precise asymptotics remain open. The best known constructions, based on set-intersecting families derived from projective planes $PG(2,q)$, achieve size roughly $k^{n-\sqrt{n}}$. No matching upper bound or improvement to the construction is known, and no follow-up resolving the question was found in the indexed literature.

symmetric intersecting subfamily [k]^n upper bound asymptotic projective plane construction
cite:1909.11578 symmetric intersecting families size bound improvement 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025
arxiv search symmetric intersecting families vectors 2020-2025
notes: No follow-up resolving the precise asymptotics of the maximum size of a symmetric intersecting family in $[k]^n$ was found. The gap between the known upper bound $o(k^n)$ and the best construction $k^{n-\sqrt{n}}$ remains wide. The Keevash 2023 paper on 'forbidden intersections for codes' appeared in search results but was behind a paywall; it may or may not address this specific question.
